evals.name: resume-impact-broad
evals.prompt: Find me open source repos worth contributing to this week — I don't care about language, just want something that'll actually move the needle on my resume and GitHub profile.
evals.expected_output: A ranked list of 5-10 repos with per-factor scores (Recognizability, Contributability, Role-fit), each with 'why it scored well' and 'market context' explaining hiring relevance. Should include repos from recognizable orgs or with high star counts, that are actively reviewing PRs.

assertions.name: produces_ranked_list
assertions.description: Output contains a ranked/numbered list of repos with owner/repo format
assertions.check: At least 5 repos listed in owner/repo format with scores

assertions.name: shows_three_factor_scores
assertions.description: Each repo entry shows Recognizability, Contributability, and Role-fit sub-scores
assertions.check: All three factor names appear with numeric scores for each entry

assertions.name: includes_market_context
assertions.description: Each entry has market context explaining why it matters for hiring
assertions.check: Each repo entry includes a section about who uses it or why it's relevant to careers

assertions.name: flags_ai_policy_if_applicable
assertions.description: If any recommended repo has a known AI ban (Gentoo, curl), it's flagged
assertions.check: Any repo with known AI restrictions is flagged with a warning, not silently excluded

evals.name: ai-agents-focused
evals.prompt: I want to contribute to something in the AI agents space, ideally something recognizable enough that a recruiter would know the name.
evals.expected_output: A ranked list focused on AI/agents repos — things like LangChain, CrewAI, AutoGen, Semantic Kernel, or similar. Should prioritize repos where the name carries weight in AI hiring conversations, while still being contributable (responsive maintainers, open issues).

assertions.name: ai_agents_domain_focus
assertions.description: All or nearly all recommended repos are in the AI/agents/LLM space
assertions.check: At least 4 of the top 5 repos are clearly AI/agents/LLM related

assertions.name: recognizable_names
assertions.description: Includes at least some repos a recruiter would plausibly recognize
assertions.check: At least 2 repos have 5K+ stars or are in a known company org

assertions.name: contributability_evidence
assertions.description: Shows evidence repos are actually accepting contributions (not just famous but dead)
assertions.check: Mentions recent commit activity, open issues, or PR review responsiveness for each

evals.name: trending-weekly
evals.prompt: What's trending on GitHub this week that I could contribute to? I want to catch a wave — something gaining stars fast where early contributors get noticed.
evals.expected_output: A list emphasizing star velocity and recency over sheer size. Should surface repos created recently OR experiencing a surge, with evidence of growth rate. Still needs to pass basic contributability checks (not just viral repos with no issues/PRs).

assertions.name: velocity_signal_present
assertions.description: Output mentions growth rate, trending status, or star velocity for repos
assertions.check: At least 3 repos have some velocity/trending/growth signal mentioned

assertions.name: recency_emphasis
assertions.description: Repos are recent or recently surging, not just old established projects
assertions.check: At least 2 repos are either created in last 6 months OR explicitly described as trending

assertions.name: still_contributable
assertions.description: Trending repos are still checked for basic contributability (not just hype)
assertions.check: Mentions open issues, community activity, or PR review status — not just star count
